title: tui: /cat file-read path doesn't strip_unsafe before send_chat — inconsistent with /! path
description: tui/src/event.rs:1274-1277: /cat converts bytes to string with from_utf8_lossy, calls strip_unsafe, then send_chat. The /! path (line 1220-1235) does the same: from_utf8_lossy → strip_unsafe → send_chat. Both are correct. The duplication between these two code paths means a future fix to strip_unsafe ordering must be applied in two places. Consider extracting a helper fn prepare_for_broadcast(raw: &str) -> String that does the strip+truncate+strip flow, called by both /cat and /!.
